title: Canonical cross-cutting constraints that every non-trivial change must prove compliance with

plain_language.what: The project's own top-level rules, made enforceable. A change cannot land (and PCC will not let a build worker start implementing) until a preflight plan records how it honours these.
plain_language.why: The rules were already written in CLAUDE.md, but tasks could proceed and get a PASS without proving they followed them. This makes the proof mandatory and machine-checked.
plain_language.how_authority_works: This file does NOT restate the rules — it POINTS at the exact sentence in the authoritative document and carries its hash. If that sentence is reworded or removed without deliberately updating this registry, the checker fails closed.

constraints.label: Research proven prior art before building
constraints.statement: Before building something new, ASSUME the problem is already solved somewhere in the world.

constraints.label: Local/deterministic first; spend the LLM only on irreducible judgment
constraints.statement: Prefer local, deterministic tools for mechanical work; spend the LLM only on real judgment.

constraints.label: Reduce owner babysitting (the #1 rule)
constraints.statement: They exist to reduce owner babysitting — the #1 rule of this project.

not_proven: Presence + hash of the cited sentence proves the rule still exists and the registry was not silently retargeted. It does NOT prove a task's research or reasoning was intellectually complete — that remains the independent verifier's judgment.
